summary: Generate diverse ultra-modern landing-page directions for V-AEP, judge them, and synthesize one definitive design blueprint
logs: Consensus winner: DIRECTION 2: The Workforce Ledger (Editorial / Swiss Precision) — DIRECTION 2: The Workforce Ledger (Editorial / Swiss Precision):90, DIRECTION 4: Illustrated Narrative Flow — Meet the Team:89, DIRECTION 2: The Workforce Ledger — Swiss Editorial:88, Direction 3: Nightshift — Dark Premium SaaS:88, Direction 2: The Workforce Ledger — Editorial/Swiss:87, DIRECTION 3: Nightshift — Dark Premium SaaS:84, Direction 4: Illustrated Narrative Flow — recurring cast:84, DIRECTION 3: Nightshift (Dark Premium SaaS):82, DIRECTION 1: Aurora Workforce (Spatial Depth):80, Direction 1: Aurora Workforce — Spatial Depth:79, DIRECTION 4: Illustrated Narrative Flow (Character Cast):78, DIRECTION 1: Aurora Workforce — Spatial Depth:72

result.consensusWinner: DIRECTION 2: The Workforce Ledger (Editorial / Swiss Precision)

blueprint.directionName: The Workforce Ledger — Editorial Precision, Warmed (V-AEP homepage + design-system blueprint)

designSystem.palette:
Swiss annual-report base on WARM PAPER with ONE indigo structural accent + a strictly-rationed WARM human accent reserved only for the two human moments (Hire, Approval). Color occupies ~5% of page area — an event, never a texture.

PAPER & SURFACE (never pure white): paper #FAFAF8 (global bg), paper-2 #F4F3EE (alt band / sunken rows), surface #FFFFFF (raised cards + diagram panels only).
INK: ink #14151A (headlines, diagram strokes, never #000), ink-70 #4A4B54 (body/secondary), ink-40 #8A8B92 (mono captions, Fig. numbers, grid labels), ink-25 #B8B7B0 (disabled/ticks).
HAIRLINES (warm greys so seams align): line #E5E4DD (all borders + visible column rules), line-strong #D6D5CC (emphasized dividers).
INDIGO SYSTEM ACCENT (existing brand, extended): brand-50 #EEF2FF (only tinted surface — active nav pill, highlighted node, RAG citation wash), brand-100 #E0E7FF (soft fills, chip bg), brand-500 #6366F1 (gradient mid-stop, hover glow), brand-600 #4F46E5 (THE accent — primary CTA, the one live connector, links), brand-700 #4338CA (CTA hover, deep strokes), brand-900 #312E81 (deep gradient stop, footer monogram node).
WARM HUMAN ACCENT (rationed — ONLY on Hire chips/buttons + Approval moments, so warmth literally = 'a human is in the loop'): warm-400 #FF9A62 (apricot primary), warm-500 #F97316 (warm hover), warm-100 #FFF1E6 (wash behind hire/approval tokens), amber-300 #FCD34D (approval 'pending' pip + rating stars only). Coral #FB7185 used ONLY for the Decline/Hold half of an approval pair.
SUCCESS mint: mint-500 #10B981, mint-400 #34D399, mint-300 #6EE7B7 (approved checks, live/online dot).
MIDNIGHT (one inverted spread — Approvals & Security §08 only): midnight #0E1020 (bg), midnight-2 #171933 (raised card on dark), text on dark = #FAFAF8 (hi) / #A9AAC0 (dim); indigo #6366F1 + warm #FF9A62 pop against it.
GRADIENTS: g-cta = linear 100deg #4F46E5 → #6366F1 (primary button); g-hero-wash = linear 165deg #FAFAF8 0% → #EEF2FF 55% → #FFF1E6 100% (faint hero tint, prints near-white); light ambient blobs = rgba(99,102,241,.10) indigo + rgba(255,154,98,.08) apricot, screen/multiply-blended so they survive PDF print.
USAGE RULES: (1) Paper everywhere; white only for cards/diagram panels. (2) Indigo = structure & trust + the ONE live diagram connector. (3) Warm = human decision points ONLY. (4) Mint = success/live. (5) Amber = pending. (6) Coral = decline/hold half of a pair. (7) Never tint >~5% of any viewport.
designSystem.typography:
System-font-safe Swiss grotesque — zero web fonts, zero external deps.
SANS (display+text): 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, Arial, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif.
MONO (editorial captions, Fig. numbers, § indices, nav, ALL numerics/stats): ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', 'Cascadia Code', 'Roboto Mono', Menlo, Consolas, monospace.
SCALE (fluid clamp desktop→mobile): Hero display clamp(3.25rem, 8.5vw, 7rem) / 700 / tracking -0.035em / line-height 0.94; H2 section clamp(2rem, 4.2vw, 3.5rem) / 700 / -0.025em / 1.02; H3 subhead 1.5rem / 600 / 1.3; Lead 1.375rem / 400 / 1.5 / ink-70; Body 1.125rem / 400 / 1.62 / ink-70; Body-sm 1rem / 400 / 1.6; Metric numeral clamp(3rem, 6vw, 5.5rem) / 700 / tabular-nums / ink (with mono unit label beside).
MONO KICKER (every section eyebrow): 0.75rem / 600 / uppercase / letter-spacing 0.14em / ink-40 — format '§01 — EMPLOYEES'.
FIG. CAPTION: mono 0.75rem / ink-40 — 'Fig. 03 · Connector map'. NAV: mono 0.8125rem / 500 / tracking 0.04em.
DISPLAY TREATMENT: headlines flush-left, tight, DELIBERATE hand-chosen line breaks (e.g. 'Hire the / workforce that / never sleeps.'); exactly ONE keyword per headline set in brand-600 (structural) OR warm-400 (the word 'hire'). All numerals font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. Body max-width 62ch / prose block 640px. Never bold body text for emphasis — use the mono kicker or an indigo inline.

homepageSections.title: §00 Masthead / sticky nav
homepageSections.layout: Flush hairline top bar inside Container. Left: mono wordmark 'VERTICAL AI' + 4-point spark glyph + pulsing mint live-dot. Center-right: mono nav (Employees · Skills · Workflows · Pricing) with underline-wipe hover; active = brand-50 pill. Right: ghost 'Sign in' → /login, then hire-variant 'Hire an AI Employee' → /register. Transparent at top; .is-scrolled adds rgba(250,250,248,.82) + blur(8px) + bottom hairline.
homepageSections.copy: Wordmark: VERTICAL AI. Nav: Employees · Skills · Workflows · Pricing. Buttons: 'Sign in' / 'Hire an AI Employee'.

homepageSections.title: §01 Hero
homepageSections.layout: 12-col grid with visible .grid-rules + drifting hairline + two light AmbientBlobs. Cols 1–6: mono kicker, oversized flush-left headline with hand-broken lines, lead, dual CTA (hire primary + ghost secondary), mono trust micro-line. Cols 7–12: the large monoline 'Workforce Constellation' DiagramPanel (Fig.00) with orbiting skill nodes + one live indigo connector + approval diamond.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §01 — THE MANAGED AI WORKFORCE. Headline: 'Hire the workforce that never sleeps.' ('Hire' in warm-400). Lead: 'V-AEP lets you hire managed AI Employees — Recruiter, HR, Sales, Support, Finance — then equip them with Skills, brief them with your Knowledge, chain them into Workflows, and gate every risky move behind human Approvals. A whole team, reporting for duty in minutes.' CTAs: 'Hire your first AI Employee' → /register, 'Sign in' → /login. Trust line (mono): '4.2M actions run · 40+ skills · SOC 2 aligned — figures illustrative.'

homepageSections.title: §02 Index strip / roster table-of-contents
homepageSections.layout: Full-width hairline-ruled mono table, five rows: §0N | employee name | one-line role | anchor arrow. Doubles as anchor-nav to the roster. paper-2 band, top+bottom line-strong rules.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §02 — THE ROSTER, INDEXED. Rows: '§01  Ada · AI Recruiter — screens CVs, books interviews, keeps every candidate warm.' / '§02  Milo · AI HR Assistant — onboarding, policy answers, time-off, the paperwork nobody wants.' / '§03  Vex · AI Sales — qualifies leads, drafts quotes, chases follow-ups on time.' / '§04  Pip · AI Support — triages tickets, answers from your docs, escalates the hard ones.' / '§05  Nova · AI Finance — invoices, reconciliations, expense checks, month-end prep.'
homepageSections.svg: None (typographic); mono index numerals + hairline rules.

homepageSections.title: §03 The problem, illustrated (before/after diptych)
homepageSections.layout: Two side-by-side hairline monoline panels (stack on mobile). Left 'Before': human ringed by 9 mismatched app tiles + tangled connectors, desaturated. Right 'After': same human calm, sliding task cards to a tidy row of 3 AI employee nodes under a faint warm-100 wash. Center headline bridges.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §03 — THE SHIFT. Headline: 'You don’t need more tools. You need more hands.' Body: 'The average team stitches together 4–6 point tools and still does the work by hand. V-AEP replaces the pile with a hire: managed AI Employees that actually operate your tools for you — under your rules.' Panel labels: 'Fig. 01 · Before — 6 tabs, 0 done' / 'Fig. 02 · After — 1 hire, work moving'.

homepageSections.title: §04 The Roster — five AI Employees
homepageSections.layout: Editorial 12-col grid of five identical hairline RosterCards. Each: RosterAvatarFrame monoline portrait, mono role label, name, 3 sample tasks as a mono checklist, skill-chip row, hire-variant 'Hire' button. Recurring named cast = memorability graft.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §04 — MEET THE TEAM. Headline: 'Five hires. One afternoon.' Card e.g. Ada — 'AI RECRUITER. Tasks: Screen inbound CVs · Book interview slots · Send candidate updates. Ships with: Gmail, Calendar, Slack.' (repeat per employee). Each CTA: 'Hire Ada' → /register.

homepageSections.title: §05 How it works — Fig. 01–06 numbered steps
homepageSections.layout: Steps ride a horizontal ConnectorRoute spine (numbered dots 01–06). Each StepBlock alternates left/right zig-zag: big display numeral + Kicker + heading + body + per-step CTA on one side, its monoline diagram DiagramPanel on the other. Fig.01 Hire & Fig.05 Approve badges use the warm accent; the rest indigo.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §05 — HOW IT WORKS. Headline: 'Hire once. Compound forever.' (full step copy in explanationSteps). CTAs shown: Fig.01 'Hire your first AI Employee → /register', Fig.06 'See the dashboard → /login'.
homepageSections.svg: ConnectorRoute spine + one diagram per step (HireBadge, SkillsConnector, KnowledgeLattice, WorkflowMini, ApprovalGate, AnalyticsMini).

homepageSections.title: §06 Skills — the connector map (scoped & revocable)
homepageSections.layout: Split: left copy + mono integration legend; right the ConnectorMap DiagramPanel — one Employee node wired to a vertical rail of integration nodes with orthogonal connectors; ONE active edge is the live indigo dash; hovering a legend item lights its edge.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §06 — SKILLS. Headline: 'Give them the tools. Keep the keys.' Body: 'Grant Skills the way you’d grant a new hire access — toggle on Slack, Gmail, Google Calendar, Stripe and 40+ more. Every skill is scoped and revocable: an employee can only ever touch what you authorize, and you can pull access in one click.' Legend (mono): 'Slack · Gmail · Google Calendar · Stripe · Google Drive · Notion · HubSpot · Twilio'. Link: 'Browse the Skills catalog →' (Marketplace).

homepageSections.title: §07 Workflows — the directed graph
homepageSections.layout: Copy left, wide WorkflowGraph DiagramPanel right (grid-dot bg in a white panel). Left-to-right directed graph with marker arrowheads; happy path stroked live indigo; one dotted 'scheduled' edge.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §07 — WORKFLOWS. Headline: 'One employee is useful. A team is transformative.' Body: 'Compose multi-step Workflows on a visual canvas and hand work between employees automatically. New CV arrives → Ada screens it → qualified? → Scheduling books the interview → the hiring manager gets a Slack summary. Run it on a trigger, on a schedule, or on demand — 24/7.' Link: 'Explore Workflows & Scheduling →'.

homepageSections.title: §08 Approvals & Security — the inverted Midnight spread
homepageSections.layout: MidnightSection full-bleed #0E1020, light-on-dark. Two columns: left an ApprovalGate monoline diagram + concrete ApprovalCard money-moment; right the security guarantees as a mono checklist with small shield glyphs. Warm accent + amber pending pip pop against the dark.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker (dim): §08 — APPROVALS & CONTROL. Headline: 'Autonomy with a seatbelt.' Body: 'Set the line no employee crosses alone. Sending an offer, issuing a refund, posting publicly — route it to a human for one-tap approval. Every action, approved or denied or automatic, lands in an immutable audit trail you can hand to compliance.' ApprovalCard: 'APPROVAL NEEDED · AI Finance wants to send invoice $12,000 → Acme Co.' with [Hold] (coral ghost) + [Approve] (mint). Second card: 'Refund of $420 → waiting on you.' Security checklist: 'Least-privilege scoped skills · Immutable audit log · Role-based access (RBAC) · SSO/SAML · Data isolation per tenant · SOC 2 aligned (illustrative).'

homepageSections.title: §09 Knowledge (RAG) — grounded, with citations
homepageSections.layout: Copy left, KnowledgeRAG DiagramPanel right: three page rects dissolving into an embedding lattice; ONE query ray traced back to a highlighted source page in brand-600 (citation graft). paper-2 band.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §09 — KNOWLEDGE. Headline: 'Answers from your playbook — not the open internet.' Body: 'Upload PDFs, docs, price lists and past tickets. We chunk, embed and index everything into private Knowledge, so your employees answer grounded in your business — always on-brand, always with a citation back to the source.' Callout (mono): 'Fig. 08 · query → embedding → cited source'.

homepageSections.title: §10 Marketplace — contact sheet
homepageSections.layout: 'Contact sheet' 3×2 grid of identical hairline-framed MarketplaceTiles, each a tiny distinct monoline mark + mono caption; one tile marked 'Installed' in indigo, last tile a dashed '+ Hire your own'. Staggered hover-lift.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §10 — MARKETPLACE. Headline: 'Prebuilt teammates, ready to install.' Body: 'Start from a proven employee or skill pack and customize from there.' Tiles: 'Recruiter Pack (Installed) · Support Desk · Sales SDR · Finance Ops · HR Onboarding · + Hire your own'. Link: 'Open the Marketplace →'.

homepageSections.title: §11 Analytics — thin-line proof
homepageSections.layout: Copy left with big mono metric readouts, AnalyticsSpot DiagramPanel right: L-axis + single polyline with vertex markers + faint indigo area fill + an annotation callout pinned to a vertex.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §11 — ANALYTICS. Headline: 'Measure output. Promote what works.' Body: 'Watch your AI workforce on the dashboard — tasks completed, hours saved, approval turnaround. Clone a top performer and scale the team the way you’d grow a department.' Metrics (mono, illustrative): '+38% tasks/wk · 312 hrs saved/mo · 2.4h avg approval SLA'.

homepageSections.title: §12 Pull-quote / proof
homepageSections.layout: Full-width editorial pull-quote in oversized display type, hairline top rule, small mono attribution + a tiny avatar-node glyph.
homepageSections.copy: Quote: '“We replaced six tabs and two contractors with three AI Employees. The Approvals gate is why our CFO signed off.”' Attribution (mono): 'ILLUSTRATIVE — HEAD OF OPERATIONS, 40-PERSON SAAS.'

homepageSections.title: §13 Final CTA
homepageSections.layout: Restated flush-left headline + hire primary + ghost secondary CTA, plus a compact 'what you get' hairline checklist (3 items). Faint g-hero-wash + grid rules echo the hero to bookend the page.
homepageSections.copy: Kicker: §13 — GET STARTED. Headline: 'Your next hire starts today.' Checklist (mono): 'Live in minutes · Scoped, revocable skills · Full audit trail'. CTAs: 'Hire your first AI Employee' → /register (hire variant), 'Sign in' → /login. Reassurance: 'No card to start · Cancel anytime · Your data stays yours.'

homepageSections.title: §14 Footer
homepageSections.layout: Fine multi-column hairline grid: Product / Platform / Company / Legal columns of mono links, mono copyright row, and the grid-constructed 'V' monogram (converging hairlines meeting at a single indigo node) as the page full-stop.
homepageSections.copy: Product: Employees, Skills, Workflows, Scheduling, Approvals, Knowledge. Platform: Marketplace, Analytics, Billing, Team, Security. Company: About, Careers, Blog, Contact. Legal: Privacy, Terms, DPA, Status. Copyright (mono): '© 2026 VERTICAL AI · V-AEP.'

explanationSteps.title: Hire an Employee
explanationSteps.body: Start like you’d start with a new hire — pick the role. Choose from AI Recruiter, HR Assistant, Sales, Support or Finance. Each arrives pre-trained on its craft, reporting for duty in minutes, not onboarding weeks. Name them, set their working hours, assign an owner. No seats to provision, no models to wire up. CTA: Hire your first AI Employee → /register
explanationSteps.svg: HireBadge (warm accent) — a monoline employee node dropping into an org-chart slot with a warm-100 wash + a '+ hired' warm chip; the ONE warm step badge.

explanationSteps.title: Grant Skills
explanationSteps.body: Give your employee the tools its human counterpart would need. Connect Slack, Gmail, Google Calendar, Stripe and dozens more with one click — every skill is scoped, so an employee can only ever touch what you authorize, and you can revoke access in one click.
explanationSteps.svg: SkillsConnector (indigo) — employee node radiating scoped connectors to skill glyphs; one live indigo dash; small 'scoped & revocable' lock ticks.

explanationSteps.title: Brief with Knowledge
explanationSteps.body: Upload your playbooks, policies, price lists and past deals. Your employee reads them into private memory (RAG) and answers grounded in your business — never generic, always on-brand, with citations back to the source.
explanationSteps.svg: KnowledgeLattice (indigo) — pages fanning into an embedding grid with one indigo citation ray.

explanationSteps.title: Chain into Workflows
explanationSteps.body: One employee is useful; a team is transformative. Compose multi-step Workflows that hand work between employees — the Recruiter screens, the HR Assistant schedules, Finance raises the offer — running on triggers or a schedule, 24/7.
explanationSteps.svg: WorkflowMini (indigo) — trigger → step → diamond branch → outcome directed graph with one animated flow dot.

explanationSteps.title: Gate with Approvals
explanationSteps.body: Set the line no employee crosses alone. Sending an offer, issuing a refund, posting publicly — route it to a human for one-tap approval. Every action is logged in an immutable audit trail you can hand to compliance. Autonomy with a seatbelt.
explanationSteps.svg: ApprovalGate (warm accent) — pipeline into a diamond checkpoint, an action token paused at a warm-100 gate with an amber pending pip, a human tap releasing a mint check; the second warm step badge.

explanationSteps.title: Measure & scale
explanationSteps.body: Watch output on the Analytics dashboard — tasks completed, hours saved, approval turnaround. Promote what works, clone a top performer, and grow your AI workforce the way you’d grow a team. CTA: See the dashboard → /login
explanationSteps.svg: AnalyticsMini (indigo) — thin-line rising polyline with vertex markers + a '+38%' annotation callout.

svgLibrary.name: Fig.00 — Hero Workforce Constellation
svgLibrary.description: Flagship monoline system diagram on a white hairline panel. Central rounded-square Employee node (monoline avatar: circle head + shoulder arc, tiny indigo on-duty dot) inside a breathing indigo status ring. Skill nodes (Slack hash, Gmail envelope, Calendar grid, Stripe bars, Knowledge book, Workflow fork) on a faint orbit that can slowly rotate around a shared transform-origin (D1 orbital graft). Six thin connectors radiate; exactly ONE is the live indigo dashed 'flow' edge. Approval diamond at top with an amber pending pip. Faint background column ticks. All strokes currentColor except the two indigo accents. Prints crisp; motion reduced-motion-guarded.

svgLibrary.name: RosterPortraits (×5, recurring cast)
svgLibrary.description: Five distinct monoline glyph 'portraits' in IDENTICAL 48×48 hairline square frames (rx14), 1.5px stroke, single indigo dot per glyph as eye/accent. Ada/Recruiter = magnifier over résumé. Milo/HR = chat bubble + heart-in-hand. Vex/Sales = upward line-chart arrow through a target ring. Pip/Support = headset ring + speech dots. Nova/Finance = ledger lines + coin. Reused across roster, index, workflow graph and final CTA so the named team reads as authored.

svgLibrary.name: ProblemDiptych
svgLibrary.description: Two hairline monoline panels. LEFT 'Before': central human glyph surrounded by 9 mismatched app-tile squares at random rotations, thin tangled connectors, all ink-40 (desaturated), one broken/dashed connector. RIGHT 'After': same human relaxed, three neat task cards sliding right (motion arrows) into a tidy row of 3 employee nodes on a faint warm-100 wash; connectors clean/orthogonal, one live indigo.

svgLibrary.name: Fig.03 ConnectorMap (Skills)
svgLibrary.description: Node-and-edge integration graph: one Employee node on the left, a vertical rail of integration nodes on the right (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Stripe, Drive, Notion), thin orthogonal connectors with small square junction dots. The hovered/active edge turns brand-600 and animates its dash. Mono labels beside each node. Small lock ticks reinforce 'scoped & revocable'.
svgLibrary.sampleSvg: <svg viewBox="0 0 420 300" role="img" aria-label="An AI Employee wired to scoped skills" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="font-family:ui-monospace,Menlo,monospace"><style>.k{stroke:#14151A;stroke-width:1.5;fill:none;stroke-linecap:round}.e{stroke:#D6D5CC;stroke-width:1.4;fill:none}.live{stroke:#4F46E5;stroke-width:1.5;fill:none;stroke-dasharray:5 6;animation:f 2.4s linear infinite}.lbl{font-size:11px;fill:#8A8B92}@keyframes f{to{stroke-dashoffset:-44}}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.live{animation:none}}</style><rect x="20" y="120" width="72" height="60" rx="12" fill="#FFFFFF" class="k"/><circle class="k" cx="56" cy="144" r="9"/><path class="k" d="M44 168 c0-12 24-12 24 0"/><path class="e" d="M92 150 H210 V50 H300"/><path class="live" d="M92 150 H210 V110 H300"/><path class="e" d="M92 150 H210 V170 H300"/><path class="e" d="M92 150 H210 V230 H300"/><g fill="#14151A"><rect x="206" y="46" width="4" height="4"/><rect x="206" y="106" width="4" height="4"/><rect x="206" y="166" width="4" height="4"/><rect x="206" y="226" width="4" height="4"/></g><g class="k"><circle cx="316" cy="50" r="14" fill="#FFF"/><circle cx="316" cy="110" r="14" fill="#EEF2FF" stroke="#4F46E5"/><circle cx="316" cy="170" r="14" fill="#FFF"/><circle cx="316" cy="230" r="14" fill="#FFF"/></g><text class="lbl" x="338" y="54">Slack</text><text class="lbl" x="338" y="114">Gmail</text><text class="lbl" x="338" y="174">Calendar</text><text class="lbl" x="338" y="234">Stripe</text></svg>

svgLibrary.name: Fig.04 WorkflowGraph
svgLibrary.description: Left-to-right directed graph on a faint grid-dot white panel: 'Trigger · New CV' → 'Ada screens' → diamond 'Qualified?' branching to 'Schedule interview' (yes) and 'Send polite decline' (no) → 'Notify hiring manager'. Arrowheads via <marker>. Dotted 'scheduled' edge; happy path stroked live indigo with a traveling flow dash. Reuse RosterPortraits inside agent nodes.
svgLibrary.sampleSvg: <svg viewBox="0 0 520 220" role="img" aria-label="A recruiting workflow graph" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="font-family:ui-monospace,Menlo,monospace"><defs><marker id="ar" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0 0l6 3-6 3z" fill="#14151A"/></marker></defs><style>.n{fill:#FFF;stroke:#14151A;stroke-width:1.5}.t{font-size:10px;fill:#14151A}.e{stroke:#14151A;stroke-width:1.5;fill:none}.live{stroke:#4F46E5;stroke-width:1.6;fill:none;stroke-dasharray:5 6;animation:f 2.4s linear infinite}@keyframes f{to{stroke-dashoffset:-44}}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.live{animation:none}}</style><rect class="n" x="8" y="90" width="78" height="40" rx="8"/><text class="t" x="18" y="106">Trigger</text><text class="t" x="18" y="120">New CV</text><path class="live" d="M86 110 H120" marker-end="url(#ar)"/><rect class="n" x="122" y="90" width="78" height="40" rx="8"/><text class="t" x="134" y="114">Ada screens</text><path class="live" d="M200 110 H236" marker-end="url(#ar)"/><rect class="n" x="236" y="86" width="48" height="48" rx="6" transform="rotate(45 260 110)"/><text class="t" x="244" y="113">Qual?</text><path class="live" d="M284 96 C320 70 330 60 360 60" marker-end="url(#ar)"/><path class="e" d="M284 124 C320 150 330 160 360 160" stroke-dasharray="3 4" marker-end="url(#ar)"/><rect class="n" x="360" y="40" width="90" height="40" rx="8"/><text class="t" x="368" y="58">Schedule</text><text class="t" x="368" y="72">interview</text><rect class="n" x="360" y="140" width="90" height="40" rx="8"/><text class="t" x="368" y="158">Polite</text><text class="t" x="368" y="172">decline</text><path class="e" d="M450 60 C480 60 480 110 486 110" marker-end="url(#ar)"/></svg>

svgLibrary.name: Fig.07 ApprovalGate (Midnight / light-on-dark)
svgLibrary.description: On the inverted #0E1020 spread: a pipeline flowing into a checkpoint — a diamond decision node with a barrier/valve symbol; an 'Action requested' token pauses at the gate with an amber-300 pending pip; a hairline human glyph taps a warm→mint approve control; a branch shows 'Approved →' continuing and 'Held' looping back. Strokes #FAFAF8, accents brand-500 + warm-400, approved check mint. Includes ripple + drawIn check motion.
svgLibrary.sampleSvg: <svg viewBox="0 0 420 220" role="img" aria-label="Risky action paused at a human approval gate" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="font-family:ui-monospace,Menlo,monospace"><style>.l{stroke:#FAFAF8;stroke-width:1.5;fill:none;stroke-linecap:round}.dim{fill:#A9AAC0;font-size:10px}.live{stroke:#6366F1;stroke-width:1.6;fill:none;stroke-dasharray:5 6;animation:f 2.4s linear infinite}.chk{stroke:#34D399;stroke-width:2;fill:none;stroke-dasharray:22;stroke-dashoffset:22;animation:draw 3s ease-in-out infinite}.rip{fill:none;stroke:#34D399;transform-origin:328px 150px;animation:rip 4s ease-out infinite}@keyframes f{to{stroke-dashoffset:-44}}@keyframes draw{40%{stroke-dashoffset:22}70%,100%{stroke-dashoffset:0}}@keyframes rip{from{transform:scale(.2);opacity:.6}to{transform:scale(2.2);opacity:0}}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.live,.chk,.rip{animation:none}.chk{stroke-dashoffset:0}}</style><rect width="420" height="220" fill="#0E1020"/><path class="live" d="M12 100 H150"/><rect x="40" y="84" width="70" height="32" rx="6" fill="#171933" stroke="#FF9A62"/><text class="dim" x="50" y="104" fill="#FF9A62">$12,000</text><circle cx="112" cy="84" r="4" fill="#FCD34D"/><rect x="150" y="74" width="52" height="52" rx="6" transform="rotate(45 176 100)" fill="#171933" stroke="#FAFAF8" stroke-width="1.5"/><path class="l" d="M164 100 h24 M176 88 v24" opacity=".7"/><text class="dim" x="158" y="150" text-anchor="middle">GATE</text><path class="l" d="M202 100 H250"/><circle cx="290" cy="150" r="16" fill="none" stroke="#FAFAF8" stroke-width="1.5"/><path class="l" d="M290 134 v-14"/><circle class="rip" cx="328" cy="150" r="14"/><circle cx="328" cy="150" r="16" fill="#171933" stroke="#34D399"/><path class="chk" d="M321 150l5 5 9 -11"/><path class="l" d="M250 92 H360" opacity=".5"/><text class="dim" x="300" y="84">Approved →</text><text class="dim" x="252" y="120">Held ↶</text></svg>

svgLibrary.name: ApprovalCard (money-moment, HTML primitive shown as SVG)
svgLibrary.description: Concrete money-moment card used in hero + §08: mono kicker 'APPROVAL NEEDED', a warm-100 'Needs approval' chip, body 'AI Finance wants to send invoice $12,000 → Acme Co.', and a button pair 'Hold' (coral ghost) + 'Approve' (mint fill) with a waiting pulse ring. Build as an HTML ApprovalCard primitive in production; SVG variant here for the hero.

svgLibrary.name: Fig.08 KnowledgeRAG
svgLibrary.description: A fan of three page rectangles (monoline text rules) on the left dissolving into a lattice/dot grid on the right (the embedding space), connected by tapering hairlines; ONE query ray traced back from the answer to a highlighted source page in brand-600 (the citation detail). Communicates 'grounded + cited'.

svgLibrary.name: Fig.10 AnalyticsSpot
svgLibrary.description: Precise thin-line chart: L-shaped axis with mono ticks, single data polyline with small circular vertex markers, a faint brand-50 area-fill under one segment, an annotation callout ('+38% tasks/wk') pinned to a vertex, baseline hairline. Reveals with a scaleY grow.

svgLibrary.name: MarketplaceContactSheet
svgLibrary.description: A 3×2 grid of identical hairline-framed tiles (contact-sheet motif), each holding a tiny distinct monoline mark + mono caption; one tile marked 'Installed' in brand-600 (brand-50 fill), the final tile a dashed-border '+ Hire your own'. Staggered hover-lift.

svgLibrary.name: SecurityShield glyphs (×5)
svgLibrary.description: Matched shield outlines each holding a mini glyph, single 1.5px weight, for the §08 checklist: check (SOC 2), lock (data isolation), fingerprint arcs (SSO/SAML), grid-of-people (RBAC), scroll (audit log). Light-on-dark inside the Midnight spread (strokes #FAFAF8, accent brand-500).
svgLibrary.sampleSvg: <svg viewBox="0 0 40 40" role="img" aria-label="Audit log" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M20 3l14 5v10c0 9-6 16-14 19-8-3-14-10-14-19V8z" fill="none" stroke="#FAFAF8" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M14 16h12M14 21h12M14 26h8" stroke="#6366F1" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg>

svgLibrary.name: ConnectorRoute (How-it-works spine)
svgLibrary.description: A long horizontal hairline 'route' behind §05 with six milestone dots numbered 01–06, a subtle dashed segment, and small tick marks like a measuring rule. The Hire (01) and Approve (05) milestone dots carry the warm accent; others indigo. Decorative structural spine.
svgLibrary.sampleSvg: <svg viewBox="0 0 600 40" role="img" aria-label="Six-step route" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="20" y1="20" x2="580" y2="20" stroke="#D6D5CC"/><g stroke="#E5E4DD"><line x1="120" y1="14" x2="120" y2="26"/><line x1="320" y1="14" x2="320" y2="26"/><line x1="460" y1="14" x2="460" y2="26"/></g><circle cx="20" cy="20" r="6" fill="#FF9A62"/><circle cx="132" cy="20" r="5" fill="#4F46E5"/><circle cx="244" cy="20" r="5" fill="#4F46E5"/><circle cx="356" cy="20" r="5" fill="#4F46E5"/><circle cx="468" cy="20" r="6" fill="#FF9A62"/><circle cx="580" cy="20" r="5" fill="#4F46E5"/></svg>

svgLibrary.name: FooterMonogram 'V'
svgLibrary.description: A large 'V' constructed purely from converging page-column hairlines meeting at a point, with a single brand-900 node at the intersection; an engineering-credibility full-stop closing the page. Grid lines fade toward the top.

svgLibrary.name: SparkGlyph (wordmark)
svgLibrary.description: A tiny 4-point diamond 'AI spark' before the VERTICAL AI wordmark; the only nav motion — twinkles (opacity+scale) on a 2.5s loop, frozen under reduced-motion. brand-600 fill.
svgLibrary.sampleSvg: <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" width="16" height="16" role="img" aria-hidden="true" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><style>.sp{animation:tw 2.5s ease-in-out infinite;transform-origin:center}@keyframes tw{0%,100%{opacity:.6;transform:scale(.9)}50%{opacity:1;transform:scale(1.1)}}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.sp{animation:none}}</style><path class="sp" d="M8 0l2 6 6 2-6 2-2 6-2-6-6-2 6-2z" fill="#4F46E5"/></svg>

svgLibrary.name: HireBadge (Fig.01 step, warm)
svgLibrary.description: The warm-accent step glyph for 'Hire': a monoline employee node dropping into an empty org-chart slot outlined with a dashed frame, a warm-100 wash filling the slot on arrival, and a warm '+ hired' chip. The ONE place a step diagram uses warm — encoding 'a human decision happened here'.
svgLibrary.sampleSvg: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 160" role="img" aria-label="Hiring an AI employee into a slot" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="font-family:ui-sans-serif,system-ui"><rect x="70" y="70" width="80" height="70" rx="10" fill="#FFF1E6" stroke="#FF9A62" stroke-dasharray="5 5"/><g stroke="#14151A" stroke-width="1.5" fill="#FFF"><rect x="78" y="20" width="64" height="56" rx="10"/><circle cx="110" cy="42" r="9"/><path d="M98 64c0-12 24-12 24 0" fill="none"/></g><path d="M110 76 v-2" stroke="#FF9A62" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="150" y="18" width="58" height="20" rx="10" fill="#FF9A62"/><text x="179" y="32" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="700" fill="#FFF">+ hired</text></svg>

svgLibrary.name: ProductSurfaceVignettes (×5, app-shell teasers)
svgLibrary.description: Compact ~200×140 monoline fake-UI vignettes teasing shipping surfaces referenced 1:1 by sections: Dashboard (avatar rows + activity dots), Analytics (rising bar+line), Scheduling (calendar grid with one mint booked slot), Team (avatar chips + role badge), Billing (a receipt with a warm total line). Flat, crisp, hairline-framed — reusable in a product-tour grid and as empty-state art inside the real app.
svgLibrary.sampleSvg: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" role="img" aria-label="Scheduling calendar with a booked slot" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="1" y="1" width="198" height="138" rx="8" fill="#FFF" stroke="#E5E4DD"/><g stroke="#E5E4DD"><line x1="20" y1="40" x2="180" y2="40"/><line x1="20" y1="70" x2="180" y2="70"/><line x1="20" y1="100" x2="180" y2="100"/><line x1="60" y1="28" x2="60" y2="120"/><line x1="100" y1="28" x2="100" y2="120"/><line x1="140" y1="28" x2="140" y2="120"/></g><rect x="104" y="74" width="32" height="22" rx="4" fill="#6EE7B7"/><text x="20" y="22" font-family="ui-monospace,Menlo,monospace" font-size="9" fill="#8A8B92">SCHEDULING</text></svg>

primitives: Container — max-w-container mx-auto px-6 wrapper; variant grid-12 exposes the 12-col CSS grid + optional GridRulesOverlay.
GridRulesOverlay — absolutely-positioned 12-col hairline overlay (repeating-linear-gradient), hero + key sections only.
Kicker — mono uppercase eyebrow '§NN — LABEL'; props index, label, tone (ink-40 | indigo | warm).
SectionHeading — flush-left H2 + optional lead; one highlighted-word slot (indigo or warm).
FigCaption — mono 'Fig. NN · Title' pinned bottom-left of any diagram panel.

Button — variants: primary (g-cta fill, shadow-cta, arrow-nudge) → /register; hire (warm-400/500 fill, shadow-warm — ONLY for Hire CTAs); ghost (hairline border, underline-wipe) → /login; link (indigo + animated underline). radius-btn, 44px min touch target.
UnderlineLink — text link with scaleX underline-wipe pseudo-element.
Card — surface #FFF, radius-card, 1px line border, shadow-card; hover → shadow-lift + border-strong. Sub-variants RosterCard, StatCard, MarketplaceTile, PlanCard.
DiagramPanel — white card sized for an inline SVG + FigCaption slot + optional faint column ticks.
Badge / Chip — pill; tones neutral (brand-100), skill (brand-50 + indigo), pending (amber-300), approved (mint), hire (warm-100 + warm-500).
StatusDot — 8px dot; online (mint-400 breathing ring), pending (amber-300), offline (ink-25).
RosterAvatarFrame — identical hairline square frame housing a per-employee monoline portrait + one indigo eye-dot.
IndexRow — §-numbered TOC row (mono §NN + name + role), doubles as anchor nav.
StepBlock — numbered How-it-works step: display numeral + Kicker + heading + body + per-step CTA + diagram; alternating reveal-l/reveal-r.
ConnectorRoute — horizontal hairline milestone spine behind the steps.
ApprovalCard — concrete money-moment card (kicker/who/what + Decline coral ghost + Approve mint fill + waiting ripple); used in §08 and hero.
MidnightSection — full-bleed inverted #0E1020 wrapper (.midnight-spread) for §08, light-on-dark tokens.
MarqueeStrip — slow logo-lozenge / mono stat ticker; pauses under prefers-reduced-motion.
Masthead / Nav — sticky bar, mono wordmark + SparkGlyph + live StatusDot, center nav, ghost + hire CTA; .is-scrolled blur.
Footer — fine multi-column hairline grid + FooterMonogram + mono copyright.
AmbientBlobs — two light-tinted drifting blobs (multiply blend) for hero atmosphere only.
Reveal — wrapper applying .reveal / .reveal-l / .reveal-r + --i stagger.
AppShell — authenticated layout: left rail nav + top bar + content well, reusing Card/Kicker/StatusDot/Button.
AuthCard — centered two-panel card for /login + /register on paper bg, with the hero constellation as the side panel.
blueprint.implementationNotes:
ORDER OF WORK: (1) Land tokens first — paste the tailwind theme.extend + globals.css; verify the visible grid overlay, reveal fallback, focus ring, and print/reduced-motion guards render before building components. (2) Build primitives bottom-up: Container/GridRulesOverlay → Kicker/SectionHeading/FigCaption → Button (all 4 variants) → Card family → Badge/StatusDot → RosterAvatarFrame → StepBlock/ConnectorRoute → ApprovalCard → MidnightSection → Masthead/Footer. (3) Author the SVG library as standalone React components in /components/svg — each a pure function returning inline <svg>, viewBox-driven, width:100%/height:auto, role+aria-label, currentColor for theme-ability. Every animated SVG carries its OWN <style> with keyframes AND its own prefers-reduced-motion guard so it is self-contained and copy-paste safe. (4) Compose sections top-to-bottom per spec; wrap each in <Reveal>. (5) Add a tiny IntersectionObserver ONLY to toggle .is-scrolled on the masthead (and optionally add a stagger class) — never as a dependency for content visibility (the @supports fallback already shows everything).

COLOR DISCIPLINE (load-bearing): warm-400/500 + warm-100 + coral + amber may appear ONLY on Hire buttons/chips, the Hire (Fig.01) and Approve (Fig.05) step badges, and Approval decision UI. Everywhere else the sole accent is indigo brand-600; mint = success/live only. Enforce with a code comment on the Button 'hire' and ApprovalCard components. This makes warmth mean 'a human is in the loop' — the strategic differentiator the judges flagged.

ONE-LIVE-CONNECTOR RULE: in any diagram exactly one path gets class 'live' (indigo dashed flow). Never animate more than one edge per figure — it is the proof-of-liveness signal and becomes noise if overused.

PRINT/PDF (this project ships a PDF): the Midnight §08 spread sets print-color-adjust:exact; blobs + grid overlays are display:none in @media print; all animation/box-shadow stripped. Because the whole system is monoline stroke SVG on paper with NO backdrop-blur/feTurbulence/glass, it prints crisp at any DPI — the deciding feasibility argument; do not regress it by adding glassmorphism.

EXTENDING — AUTH PAGES (/login, /register): reuse paper bg + g-hero-wash, the masthead wordmark, and a two-panel AuthCard: left = form in a white Card (mono field labels, Button primary 'Create account' on /register / 'Sign in' on /login, ghost cross-link, focus ring), right = the Fig.00 constellation (or a simplified single-employee node) as reassurance art with a mono caption 'Your workforce, ready in minutes.' Keep the §-kicker ('§ — CREATE ACCOUNT'). /register CTA copy stays 'Hire your first AI Employee'. Warm accent is allowed on the register submit (a hire moment); login submit stays indigo.

APP SHELL (authenticated): left rail nav (mono labels: Employees, Skills, Workflows, Scheduling, Approvals, Knowledge, Marketplace, Analytics, Billing, Team) each with a monoline glyph from the SVG library; top bar with the masthead wordmark + a global StatusDot (mint live) + an Approvals bell that shows an amber pending pip when items wait (reusing the exact §08 semantics). Content wells use the same Card/DiagramPanel/Kicker/FigCaption primitives, so a Workflow canvas in-app is visually the same object as Fig.04 on the homepage — marketing and product are one language (the 1:1 section→surface mapping graft). Employees pages reuse RosterCard + RosterAvatarFrame with the recurring named cast as seed employees.

FEATURE PAGES: each maps to a homepage section and reuses its hero diagram as the page header — Skills → Fig.03 ConnectorMap, Workflows → Fig.04 WorkflowGraph, Knowledge → Fig.08 RAG, Approvals → the Midnight treatment + ApprovalCard, Analytics → Fig.10. Empty states use ProductSurfaceVignettes. Keep §NN kickers and Fig.NN captions across the app for editorial continuity.

GOTCHAS: (a) tabular-nums is global — good for the ledger feel; confirm it does not distort prose numerals (fine for this content). (b) The grid-rules background uses a calc tied to --grid-gap/--grid-cols; if the container gap changes, update both. (c) animation-timeline:view() is progressive-enhancement only — QA with it disabled (older browsers + print) to confirm the @supports-not fallback shows all content. (d) Do NOT add framer-motion, an icon library, or image assets — every glyph is in the SVG library; new ones follow the same monoline 1.5px currentColor style. (e) When regenerating the proposal PDF or any print artifact via headless Chrome (the project's documented command), the Midnight spread is the only dark element — confirm print-color-adjust is honored so it does not render as a white box."
